content: We can frame the 2025 evolution of ICM-powered autonomous agents as a natural selection process where the comparison of ICM, RND, and general curious agents acts as a fitness filter: only curiosity mechanisms that balance sustained open-ended exploration and targeted novel-state reward generation survive to be deployed in next-generation autonomous agents. This means 2025 configurable ICM designs can borrow the adaptive intrinsic reward scaling of RND to outperform general curious agents in long-horizon autonomous tasks.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
